title: Reading Systems Workstream
subtitle: Series 03, new Reading Systems skills, and the hard boundary that keeps derivative interpretation outside the living doc.
scope: Current Reading Systems work across Series 03 articles, skill drafts, and the boundary decisions tracked in issues #120-#128.
sourceCoverage: GitHub issues #120-#128, local skill drafts under `.agents/skills`, the Series 03 article files in `docs/`, the TrackAndBack validation corpus inspected locally on 2026-04-23, one private ThreadC onboarding validation pass built from repo sources only, and one private preparation-angle validation built from the full Gertjan inbox corpus. Private drafts, derivation notes, onboarding notes, preparation notes, and advice outputs remain outside tracked docs.
objective: Make Reading Systems a real value-centered workstream: four concrete use-case essays and a small skill surface that help humans derive value from context, onboarding, and preparation while keeping every living doc centered on the work's objective and current state.
successCondition: A contributor can see the current article arc, the current skill maturity, the hard boundaries that must not drift, and the next checks needed to turn Reading Systems into a stable use-case track without re-deriving the core discipline.

objectiveFacets.name: Series arc
objectiveFacets.description: Series 03 lands as one coherent use-case arc rather than a pile of adjacent essays.

objectiveFacets.name: Skill surface
objectiveFacets.description: A small operational skill surface exists for method reading, context-derived composition, and private onboarding.

objectiveFacets.name: Objective/current-state spine
objectiveFacets.description: Every living doc in this track still starts from the work's objective and current state.

objectiveFacets.name: Derivative outside the doc
objectiveFacets.description: Method reading, control reading, and other derivative interpretation stay in sidecars, reports, or preparation passes rather than first-class doc content.

objectiveFacets.name: Human-AI collaboration
objectiveFacets.description: The articles and skills describe the same loop: AI reads broadly, humans judge and act from a bounded living-doc surface.

invariants.name: Objective and current state stay first
invariants.statement: Reading Systems outputs begin by making the work's objective and current state explicit before any method, control, or value-model reading is added.

invariants.name: Derivative interpretation never becomes first-class doc structure
invariants.statement: Method readings, control readings, and other second-order interpretations may appear in sidecar memos or preparation surfaces, but never as durable first-class content inside the living doc itself.

invariants.name: Use cases still produce normal living docs
invariants.statement: Context-derived, onboarding, and preparation use cases still aim at the same kind of value-centered living doc; only the surrounding assistance and sidecar outputs change.

callouts.title: How to read this doc
items: Status Snapshot is the landing view — where the Reading Systems effort stands right now.
Objective Coherence projects the current facets, coverage, and invariants from the doc root.
Series 03 Narrative Arc tracks the current article set as one content lane.
Reading Systems Skill Surface tracks the operational tool layer used to derive value from the angle.
Hard Boundaries and Next Checks carry the decisions and follow-up work that keep the track disciplined.

sections.title: Status Snapshot
sections.rationale: A compact opening view of where the Reading Systems effort stands across the local article drafts, the drafted skills, and the newly clarified boundary.

callout.title: Current edge
items: Series 03 now has four published use-case essays: method, context, onboarding, and preparation.
The three Reading Systems skills now have a first re-anchor pass: objective first, current state second, derivative output outside the living doc.
The hard boundary is now explicit: derivative interpretation may help people derive value, but it does not become first-class living-doc content.
A first corrective pass is now woven into the essays themselves: the Reading Systems effort needed its own living doc once the core started drifting.
The split is now validated once on a real corpus: Gertjan's TrackAndBack marketing doc stayed clean while the method reading lived in a separate sidecar page.
A private context-derived draft now proves the normal-doc path on that same corpus: objective, current state, proof surface, and next value moves stayed inside the doc; derivation stayed outside it.

A second private validation now proves the onboarding path on a real repo: a ThreadC partner-integration onboarding draft stayed work-centered while the broader onboarding read remained in a separate note.
That ThreadC onboarding draft is already useful in two ways: as a scoped delivery surface for the first integration discussion and as the map for where deeper exploration should go next.

A third private validation now proves the preparation angle on the whole Gertjan inbox corpus: the resulting preparation doc stayed work-centered while the broader corpus reading remained in a separate note.
Taken together, those three passes now count as a complete Reading Systems test line: all three use-case angles converge on a normal living doc while the broader reading stays outside it.
The method-reading skill now also supports optional risk, solution-space, convergence-advice, and governance-advice layers, demonstrated on that same corpus.
The final Series 03 prose pass is now landed across all four published pieces: objective and current state are explicit as the spine, and sidecar-only readings are described consistently.
With the test line complete and the series published, next work is use, iteration, and deciding which later sidecar examples deserve public treatment.

sections.title: Series 03 Narrative Arc
sections.rationale: The Reading Systems effort is currently expressed as a coherent four-piece article arc plus one explicit revision pass, so content-production is the right parallel surface for the editorial lane.

data.name: What a living doc reveals about how you think, structure, and create value
data.synopsis: Establishes the reading frame: a living doc reveals ontology, control, evidence, and value assumptions because structure reveals what the author thinks the work is.
dependencies: Sets the base reading frame for the rest of Series 03.
notes: File: docs/blog-what-living-docs-reveal.html.
Current state: published and visible in the public Writing index.
Current follow-up: keep objective + current state visibly primary, with method reading clearly downstream.
A new paragraph now makes the correction explicit: even this series needed a living doc once the core started drifting.

data.name: Building a living doc from context, before anyone adopts the system
data.synopsis: Introduces the pre-adoption modeling use case: build a first living doc from authorized context before the subject maintains one themselves.
dependencies: Builds on the reading frame from №01.
notes: File: docs/blog-context-derived-living-docs.html.
Current state: published and visible in the public Writing index.
Current follow-up: keep the resulting living doc normal and value-centered; any derivative reading remains outside the doc.
A new paragraph now uses the Reading Systems workstream itself as the concrete proof that the doc exposes drift when the model starts floating away from the work.

data.name: The fastest onboarding doc is the one you build for yourself
data.synopsis: Applies the same model to legitimate post-access onboarding: use a private living doc to shorten time to understanding and first value.
dependencies: Builds on the context-derived composition idea from №02.
notes: File: docs/blog-private-onboarding-living-doc.html.
Current state: published and visible in the public Writing index.
Current follow-up: keep onboarding value loops and organizational fit as support around the core doc, not as first-class doc structure.
A new paragraph now ties the use case back to the Reading Systems correction: without the doc, understanding can become articulate before it becomes useful.

data.name: Come prepared to the table
data.synopsis: Extends the use case to serious conversations — partnership, negotiation, advisory, or collaboration — where arriving with a real model changes the quality of the table.
dependencies: Builds on the same preparation logic as №02 and №03.
notes: File: docs/blog-come-prepared-to-the-table.html.
Current state: published and visible in the public Writing index.
Current follow-up: keep preparation as a use case around the doc rather than letting preparation logic become the doc's subject.
A new paragraph now states the same lesson in preparation terms: interpretation only stays useful when it is held against the work.

data.name: Series 03 re-anchor pass on objective + current state
data.synopsis: Cross-piece edit pass that makes the same model explicit across all four essays: objective and current state stay first; derivative readings remain downstream support.
dependencies: Depends on #128 clarifying the boundary and #127 applying it across Series 03.
notes: Tickets: #127 and #128.
This editorial follow-up is now landed in the prose itself.
The current pass explicitly says why the Reading Systems effort needed its own living doc: the core started drifting while the essays and skills were becoming more articulate.
The four drafts now describe the same split consistently: normal living docs stay about the work, and broader readings stay in sidecars.

sections.title: Reading Systems Skill Surface
sections.rationale: The current implementation lane is a small operational tool layer rather than a product surface: support skills already exist and the new Reading Systems skills are still prototype instruments.

data.name: /living-doc bootstrap
data.purpose: Connect a session to the relevant living docs, assess freshness, and keep the current work grounded in the existing doc surface.
data.whenToUse: Use when the session needs to attach itself to a real living doc before making changes or reading state.
caveats: Bootstrap only. It does not replace the need for a normal work-centered living doc.

data.name: /crystallize
data.purpose: Derive the governance layer from a doc's stable shape so the objective facets, coverage edges, invariants, and fingerprint remain inspectable.
data.whenToUse: Use after a doc's main shape is stable enough that the meta layer can be trusted.
caveats: Second-pass refinement only. It should not be used to smuggle derivative interpretation into the doc.

data.name: /explainability-sync
data.purpose: Produce one extremely short explanation of the real objective and the current state so a fresh session does not misread the doc's main purpose.
data.whenToUse: Use when a doc needs one compact objective/current-state reading inside the normal living-doc shape.
caveats: Hard limit: objective and current state only. It is not a free-form interpretation layer.

data.name: /method-reading
data.purpose: Read a living doc as a method surface and surface objective, current state, ontology, control, evidence, value, and optional risk, solution-space, convergence-advice, and governance-advice layers as sidecar output.
data.whenToUse: Use when the user wants to understand how a document thinks work becomes coherent, not to rewrite the doc itself.
caveats: Objective and current state now come first in the contract.
Risk assessment, pivot options, convergence advice, and governance advice must remain sidecar output and stay constructive rather than performatively critical.
Existing registry types should be preferred before suggesting a new convergence type.

data.name: /context-derived-doc
data.purpose: Compose a first living doc from authorized context before native adoption, while keeping the resulting doc about the work rather than about our interpretation of it.
data.whenToUse: Use when enough source material exists to model a method, system, workstream, or value loop before the subject maintains a living doc themselves.
caveats: The resulting living doc should stay normal and work-centered.
Any derivation memo or reading report belongs outside the living doc.
notes: Now privately validated once on the TrackAndBack corpus: the draft converged on a normal living doc rather than a derivative reading artifact.

data.name: /private-onboarding
data.purpose: Build a private onboarding living doc from granted sources so a new employee, contractor, or freelancer can orient fast and find first value loops.
data.whenToUse: Use after contracts are signed, access is granted, and the person needs a fast path from sources to useful contribution.
caveats: The onboarding reading can help derive value, but it should not become first-class living-doc structure.
Any onboarding memo or help-surface analysis belongs in sidecar output, not the core doc.
notes: Now privately validated once on a real repo via a ThreadC partner-integration onboarding pass: the draft stayed a normal living doc and the broader onboarding read stayed in a separate note.
That validation also proved practical usefulness: the resulting doc already works as both a delivery surface and the starting map for deeper exploration.

sections.title: Hard Boundaries
sections.rationale: These are now explicit ground-truth calls that keep the Reading Systems effort from drifting into a meta-framework detached from normal living-doc discipline.

data.name: Objective and current state remain the spine
notes: The core living doc still answers: what is the objective of the work, and what is the current state relative to that objective.
Everything else is downstream of that base reading.

data.name: Derivative interpretation stays outside the living doc
notes: Method reading, control-model reading, value-model reading, and similar second-order interpretation may help people derive value from the angle, but they never become first-class living-doc structure.
If these readings are useful, they live in a sidecar memo, reading report, or preparation surface.

data.name: Skills may emit sidecars, never derivative doc structure
notes: Reading Systems skills can produce value-bearing sidecar outputs such as preparation notes, method readings, or onboarding memos.
They must not persist that derivative layer into the living doc itself.

data.name: Context, onboarding, and preparation still aim at the same kind of doc
notes: These use cases vary the angle and the surrounding assistance, not the species of living doc being created.
The living doc remains about the work, its state, its evidence, and the next value-bearing moves.

sections.title: Next Checks
sections.rationale: The immediate work is not more ideation; it is a compact set of checks that re-anchor the drafted skills and the article set on the newly explicit boundary.

data.name: Re-anchor skill outputs on objective + current state
notes: A first pass is now landed in all three Reading Systems skill drafts: /method-reading, /context-derived-doc, and /private-onboarding.
The explicit correction is now in the contracts: objective read first, current state read second, derivative help only as sidecar output.
The /method-reading draft now also supports optional risk, solution-space, convergence-advice, and governance-advice passes on top of the same sidecar boundary.
Remaining work is validation and polish, not the initial boundary correction itself.

data.name: Re-anchor the Series 03 essays on the same model
notes: The final corrective pass is now woven into the four article drafts, using the Reading Systems workstream itself as the concrete example.
The article set now repeats the same objective/current-state discipline consistently across method, context, onboarding, and preparation.
The essays now make the surrounding human-AI help legible without letting that become the subject of the living doc.

data.name: Validate the sidecar-output shape on one real corpus
notes: Validated on Gertjan's TrackAndBack corpus.
The method sidecar, advice reading, and Dutch advice note now live only in a local private folder outside tracked docs.
Tracked materials keep the validation result, not the private file paths.
The point was to prove the support layer helps without contaminating the doc surface, and this first pass does that cleanly.

data.name: Validate that all three use cases still converge on a normal living doc
notes: The context-derived path is now proven once on the TrackAndBack corpus via a private draft living doc plus a separate private derivation note.
That draft stayed in the normal shape: objective, current state, proof surface, current choices, and next value moves.
The derivative note remained outside the doc, which keeps the boundary clean.
The private-onboarding path is now also proven once via a private ThreadC partner-integration onboarding draft plus a separate onboarding note.
That onboarding draft stayed in the normal shape: objective, canonical work surfaces, proof and quality bar, coordination rhythm, and first value loops.
The broader onboarding read remained outside the doc, which keeps the same boundary intact on a second real case.
The resulting onboarding doc is already usable as a real delivery artifact and as the entry point for follow-on exploration, which is stronger proof than format compliance alone.
The preparation angle is now also proven once via a private corpus doc built from the full Gertjan inbox plus a separate preparation note.
That preparation doc stayed in the normal shape: objective, corpus families, explicit-readiness checkpoints, and next preparation moves.
The broader corpus reading remained outside the doc, which keeps the same boundary intact on the third angle as well.
Taken together, these three validations should now be treated as one completed test of the Reading Systems rule rather than as isolated examples.
Context-derived, onboarding, and preparation should all still produce the same kind of value-centered living doc.
Only the surrounding assistance and sidecar outputs should vary by angle.
